title: carex peregrina
intro: Carex peregrina, also known as the "dwarf bluejoint sedge," is a perennial grass-like plant that belongs to the sedge family. Traditionally, Carex peregrina has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory conditions, and supporting the immune system. Carex peregrina is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies in the form of tinctures and salves.
